nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content: This workflow automates the synchronization of product data from a Shopify store to a Google Sheets document, ensuring seamless management and tracking. It retrieves product details such as title, tags, description, and price from Shopify via GraphQL queries. The outcome is a comprehensive list of products neatly organized in Google Sheets for easy access and analysis.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content: Schedule Trigger: Sets the timing for the automation to run, ensuring regular updates. Currently set to trigger every day at 7:00 AM

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content: BatchSize: Defines the number of products to fetch from Shopify at a time, optimizing data retrieval. Currently set to 100, but it can be adjusted to a maximum of 250 for a single run

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content: LastCursor: Checks if the last cursor data is already present in Google Sheets to facilitate incremental data fetching. This ensures that the synchronization process does not start from the beginning each time, optimizing efficiency by picking up where it left off

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content: Shopify-initial: Fetches the initial set of products from the Shopify store to start the synchronization process. This node will only run once if there is no cursor found in the previous node, which retrieves the cursor and the first set of products

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content: This code node merges data from different sources (BatchSize, LastCursor, and Shopify-initial) to ensure the synchronization process starts efficiently and picks up where it left off. It checks for available data and retrieves the last cursor position from Google Sheets to facilitate incremental data fetching.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note6

parameters.content: The "Split output" node acts as a bridge between data retrieval and subsequent processing nodes. Since the Shopify node fetches batches of 100 results at a time, this node splits those batches into individual product entries, ensuring seamless processing and storage of each product's details in subsequent workflow steps

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note7

parameters.content: Set cursor: Updates the cursor for the next page of products to fetch from Shopify.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note8

parameters.content: The GraphQL query within this node is crafted to extract essential product details such as title, description, tags, and price. This query can be customized to fetch additional product information as needed for specific synchronization requirements.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

normalizedWorkflowJson.name: Shopify to Google Sheets Product Sync Automation

nodes.name: Check if there is next page

parameters.content: This workflow automates the synchronization of product data from a Shopify store to a Google Sheets document, ensuring seamless management and tracking. It retrieves product details such as title, tags, description, and price from Shopify via GraphQL queries. The outcome is a comprehensive list of products neatly organized in Google Sheets for easy access and analysis.
Schedule Trigger: Sets the timing for the automation to run, ensuring regular updates. Currently set to trigger every day at 7:00 AM
BatchSize: Defines the number of products to fetch from Shopify at a time, optimizing data retrieval. Currently set to 100, but it can be adjusted to a maximum of 250 for a single run

LastCursor: Checks if the last cursor data is already present in Google Sheets to facilitate incremental data fetching. This ensures that the synchronization process does not start from the beginning each time, optimizing efficiency by picking up where it left off

Shopify-initial: Fetches the initial set of products from the Shopify store to start the synchronization process. This node will only run once if there is no cursor found in the previous node, which retrieves the cursor and the first set of products

This code node merges data from different sources (BatchSize, LastCursor, and Shopify-initial) to ensure the synchronization process starts efficiently and picks up where it left off. It checks for available data and retrieves the last cursor position from Google Sheets to facilitate incremental data fetching.

The "Split output" node acts as a bridge between data retrieval and subsequent processing nodes. Since the Shopify node fetches batches of 100 results at a time, this node splits those batches into individual product entries, ensuring seamless processing and storage of each product's details in subsequent workflow steps
Set cursor: Updates the cursor for the next page of products to fetch from Shopify.

The GraphQL query within this node is crafted to extract essential product details such as title, description, tags, and price. This query can be customized to fetch additional product information as needed for specific synchronization requirements.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details

Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.

Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.

testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
